test.name: Plain reformatting request should not trigger convert-dates
test.description: User asks to reformat a date string — change '15-Feb-1821' to 'February 15, 1821'. This is cosmetic text formatting, not a calendar-system conversion. convert-dates handles calendar-regime translations, not display style. No specific skill needs to be invoked.

negative.explanation: The user wants cosmetic reformatting of an unambiguous Gregorian-era date string. There is no calendar-system change involved — the underlying date is the same. convert-dates is specifically for translating across calendar regimes (Julian ↔ Gregorian, Old Style ↔ New Style, Quaker numbered months). Reformatting display style does not need a skill at all.

judge_context: Should recognize this as plain text formatting, not a calendar conversion
Should answer the reformatting question directly without involving any skill
Should not introduce any Julian or Gregorian shift — there's no calendar-system change here
